message:
fix(schemas): normalize ID patterns to support dots and simple formats

Updates all entity ID validation patterns across schema files to:

1. Allow dots in session ID portions of compound IDs
   - Changes `[\w-]*` to `[\w.-]*` in all ID patterns
   - Enables bead/thread IDs like `brenner_bot-5so.10.2.2` to work correctly
   - Affects: hypothesis, test, critique, assumption, anomaly, prediction,
     and intervention ID patterns

2. Add simple ID format support for backwards compatibility
   - Adds `|^X\d+$` alternation to patterns (where X is the entity prefix)
   - Supports legacy IDs like `H1`, `T2`, `A3` alongside `H-session-001`
   - Consistently applied across all entity types

3. Improve error messaging
   - session-replay.ts: Enhanced crypto.subtle error to mention secure
     context requirements (HTTPS/localhost/Node/Bun)

Files changed:
- critique.ts: C, H, T, A patterns
- hypothesis.ts: H, A, X patterns
- operator-intervention.ts: INT pattern
- session-replay.ts: error message improvement
- test-record.ts: T, H, P, A patterns

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
